description: Raskol gangs are criminal groups in Papua New Guinea, primarily in urban areas like Port Moresby and Lae. They emerged in the 1970s from squatter settlements, engaging in theft, drug trafficking, and violence. Initiation often involves rape. They remain active, with thousands of members.

country_links.evidence_quote: serving as middlemen in the marijuana trade both within PNG and between PNG and Australia
country_links.context: Raskol gangs serve as middlemen in the marijuana trade between Papua New Guinea and Australia.
